question: Paul criticizes believers for taking each other to court. How does he describe the mere fact that they have lawsuits with one another?

choices.A: It is already a defeat for them
choices.B: It is neutral as long as the judge is fair
choices.C: It is required to protect church purity
choices.D: It is acceptable only for business disputes

teaching.title: Lawsuits as a defeat
teaching.body: Paul states that the very existence of lawsuits among believers is already a defeat, and he urges them toward suffering wrong rather than doing wrong.
